lines.text: {PlayerName}... {PlayerName}...
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Oh, thank goodness... You're finally awake!
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: How are you feeling? Any headache? Tingling feet? Upset stomach?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Phew... I tried everything, but you just wouldn't wake up. I thought you were...
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Wait a second, did you actually fall asleep listening to my music? Oh... It kind of breaks my heart to know you reacted like that when it wasn't even a lullaby.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Helpful for your sleeping troubles, hmm?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Oh...
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: So my Forte created a special connection that led to some unexpected developments...
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: The girl, is she friend or foe...? What truths lie behind the seal...? And who is that mysterious wandering figure...?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I have a feeling this is going to be a very exciting song!
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Ahem... I'll pass this information on to Lady Carlotta. So, what do you plan to do next?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Didn't expect the head of the Fisalia family to come looking for you, especially while they're still in the middle of negotiations with Lady Carlotta.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: She's just as unpredictable as the rumors say.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I suppose you would want to know more about her before taking up her invitation, hmm?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: She's known to be a recluse and rarely makes public appearances. No one outside the family even knows her real name. They just call her the "head of the Fisalia family."
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I've heard quite a few rumors. Which one would you like to hear first?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I have "Shadow of the Ancient," "Lady Bane," or maybe "Entangler of Hearts"?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Hello... That's why they're called rumors. I'm just doing my due diligence by being faithful to the source material.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: So, ready to dive right in?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Well... Long story short, the Fisalia family was one of the first to set foot on these islands and establish order in Rinascita.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Their lineage even predates the Order of the Deep, and their craft of extracting toxins from aquatic plants is a legacy passed down through generations.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Word is, they select the new head of household by having candidates ingest each other's poisons and wait to see who's still breathing when it's all over.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Stories about the current head of the Fisalias get even wilder. They say she didn't go through the usual selection process. Instead, she took the throne by poisoning her predecessor.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Others say she's a master in the art of manipulation, drowning her victims in hallucinations until they agree to her terms without even realizing it.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: In any case, the Fisalia family aside, she's someone you definitely want to handle with care.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: If you're set on meeting her, I'd be extra careful if I were you.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Salutations, {PlayerName}. My name is Ciaccona, a wandering bard. I'm glad to have met you.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: I was sent by Lady Carlotta to find you. As I passed that abandoned house, I suddenly felt this... well, you know, rush of inspiration that just can't be ignored! So, I stopped to compose a piece...
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Surprise, surprise, the melody led me right to you.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Well... I suppose this won't count as me getting distracted, right?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Lady Carlotta told me you encountered the supposed Martyred Maiden—Cartethyia.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: She dispatched search teams soon after, but there's been no sign of Cartethyia since.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: That's why she asked me to use my Forte to recreate the frequency you experienced at the time, to see if any details you might have missed become clearer.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Well, it's a little tricky to explain in words... Think of it like being in an immersive portrait. Can you tell me about your meeting with Cartethyia?
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: The more details the better. Even the smallest things count.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Let's see here... Tweak a few notes here. Some emotions there. Textures... That should do it.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: Now then, {PlayerName}. Time for you to "tune in" to your memories.
lines.quest_name: The Maiden, The Defier, The Death Crier

lines.text: {PlayerName}, Carlotta, it's been too long!
Oh, I remember it well! Those two guards of yours were like giants from Septimont, easily two hundred pounds of pure muscle!
How should I put it... Well, as they say, families are always rising and falling.
But a song is a song whether sung in a grand hall or beneath the open sky. As for family matters... let the past stay where it belongs.
Anyway, the two of you came in such a hurry. I suppose you're not here to take in the music.
Oh so... So you eavesdropped on a man rambling on in C3-sharp, and then poof, out pops a "monster"?
It is the point! I need to know what kind of music could possibly have the power to conjure up those "monsters" of yours.
Let me see... it should sound something like this! About that sharp, {PlayerName}.
I know right? We should suggest to Ms. Margherita that she add new signage: "Sing with civility, it starts with us!"
Of course. But I still need to know what kind of music could possibly have the power to conjure up those "monsters" of yours.
The Golden Anthem... that name should never be spoken.
They're criminals. To conspire with them is to risk banishment at sea, or worse.
...Fine.
From what you've told me, the composers of this poem cursed Rinascita spelling the fall of Ragunna.
When the poem is played some type of horror appears. If I understand correctly, that's what attacked you and Carlotta.
If you ask me this story is a right load of codswallop. I don't believe a simple little poem could curse Rinascita.
We need to get to the heart of this poem and music mystery. Let me join you. I can't resist a good adventure.
I'll help you find the link between the music and those Daemons. But in return, I want you to hand these pages to me for safekeeping.
Then we have a deal!
Since you believe this music summons them, how about we test it for ourselves? I've already memorized the sheet music.
The final work of poets banished to distant seas and condemned to death...
What, I wonder, did the verses forged in flames truly sing of?
Ta-da! Welcome to <te href=800010>Requiem Paradise</te>!
This is where Ragunna's musicians lay their old instruments to rest.
For the people of Ragunna, the sea is everything. We live and sleep by the sea, and our souls drift out to sea when we pass.
Since an instrument is an extension of the musician, it's only fitting that it's buried by the sea. May its dreams be long and sweet before returning to the waves.
I'm certain this is where the poets of the Golden Anthem laid their instruments to rest before their banishment.
What's wrong? You seem troubled.
Is that what's been on your mind this whole time? I say, if the answers aren't coming, let it all go and clear your mind.
To be honest... I haven't found any leads either. But worry not, this is what we bards call a "bottleneck."
Once, I spent three days just trying to find the right words to describe a tree... couldn't do it. Not one word.
So I stood under that tree and prayed for an apple to fall on my head.
You know, just like in those stories where inspiration strikes after an apple hits you. And, well... at least you'd get a snack out of it.
Sadly, the apples weren't ripe and none fell. No free nibbles for me, unfortunately.
The apples never fell. I stood under that tree until I couldn't any longer, but still no inspiration came.
Try it!
Later, when I finally decided to leave, I looked up through the gaps in the branches and suddenly saw a blooming flower atop a cliff.
Turns out, even without an apple, inspiration may yet strike. But if I hadn't stood there, I might've missed that unexpected burst of beauty.
So… maybe this is enough. Let's put aside heavy thoughts and continue our walk with a clear mind.
Is that so? But I'm nowhere near as dazzling as the sun. If anything, I'd rather be like Icarus in the story.
Soaring freely in the sky, even if it means... falling in the end.
A penniless bard and the Laureate roving in a foreign land. Together, we rise up against the Order of the Deep itself...
But as long as the seeds of doubt are planted, the kindling in Ragunna will eventually ignite to forge its true gold.
There. Now how's that for a story worth telling?
Ah! Speaking of which... Have you ever read that novel? It's about a knight on a quest to challenge a giant in the name of justice.
The knight is also accompanied by a squire. Time after time, they throw themselves at the seemingly invincible giant!
I am no Montelli, Acolyte of the Order of the Deep, or Knight of Ragunna...
But still, I shall lead the charge! Ya-hoo! Mount up, Sir Knight! To battle we go!
Phew...
A masked hero suddenly appeared, rescuing the bard right when she was about to be apprehended by the Order.
But that's a story for another time.
You know what this means for you, right?
Even with a mask on, people will still recognize the Laureate.
I'm now a wanted criminal by the Order.
Hahaha...
Now this is what a real poet... No, wait, a real knight is supposed to be!
Well then...
Do you feel it? The breeze from Whisperwind Haven. You can even catch the faint scent of wet grass.
Next thing you know, maybe an apple will fall on our heads. Or maybe a big gray wolf will leap out and chase a squirrel down a rabbit hole...
Thank you, {PlayerName}.
I don't have the Blessed Maiden's miracles, nor the powers of a hero. I'm just a wandering bard.
The only thing I can offer is to immortalize this moment. I will carry all the stories about you with me...
By the way, I met a man at Margherita's who claims to be a playwright. You should watch out for him!
I'm not entirely sure who he really is... But my guess is that he might be the Resonator who's been working with the Order.
Here, you can have this music sheet! I'm heading off on a new mission!
An escape mission! Starting today, I'm no longer just a wandering bard. I've got the Order hunting me now! A journey like this? It's begging to be written into a story.
Don't you worry! I still remember our promise.
About the song I wrote you... We will meet again, and I'll sing it for you, I swear!
After all, we're partners in crime now!
These voices... they're people of Ragunna, aren't they? I think I heard Mr. C3-sharp in the mix.
Perhaps the voices themselves are what causes the Daemons to manifest.
In the legends, Daemons are born of death and curses.
But what we've seen isn't like that, no. They're more like horrors created from peoples' words and imagination.
Of course, I'm just speculating. The voices were somewhat muddled. Perhaps I can refine the melody. This time we need to listen harder.
Let's find out what the true story really is...
These "Bonfire Poets" were no doubt members of the Poets Society. The note says they had wandered the area looking for inspiration.
To find their melody, we should try to follow the route they once took.
Perhaps inspiration will strike us along the way!
This was the last stop on their route. Oh, the view just now as we soared in the sky was just breathtaking!
What is it? Has anything come to mind?
Oh yes, how did I miss that. The wind is the finest recorder of melodies.
It rises from the primal, churning endlessly, echoing in our ears without end. I believe the melodies left by the Poets Society were witnessed by the wind, and it will carry them to us.
Playing a tune, of course. I want to capture the mood they must have felt when they were here... I'm curious as to what they left behind.
I never thought his words would reach into the depths I sealed away so long ago.
This is... the past.
Is that what you're trying to tell me with your play...? But you know nothing of how the Golden Anthem responded.
Hear that? The bell they tolled... I will be the one to carry its sound forward.
You're nothing but a figment of my imagination. None of this concerns you. It was a price we were willing to pay. We have no regrets.
When I was a child, I lacked the ability to prove the Sentinel's true form. But now, with the Inverted Tower shifting and the prophesied Laureate among us...
I believe the opportunity has presented itself for the truth to be—
The timbre also matches... I believe this lute is exactly what we've been looking for.
Sometimes, even when the instruments are the same, the performer's mood can change the entire sound.
For example, this lute doesn't seek to boast. It speaks softly, like a traveler sharing a story over tea in the warmth of an afternoon.
The true purpose of music is to express what words cannot.
{PlayerName}, if there's something you want to say but can't put into words, if you sing it, maybe I'll understand.
Me? Oh, perhaps another time. Once all this is settled, I'll perform for you. Be sure to listen closely then!
Now, it's in the lute's hands. I hope this melody holds a tale worth telling.
Is this form of the Sentinel the same as the one you saw at Avinoleum, {PlayerName}?
So the poets were indeed banished for uncovering the Order's secrets.
Even under interrogation, they held their silence. It wasn't until they met this mysterious man and learned the truth that they chose to become "Knights of Ragunna."
Resentment and curses are not songs of their own style. As for the true mastermind, we still have no direct proof linking him to the Order.
We must confront his lies and put an end to these Daemon incidents...
But once the truth of the Golden Anthem and Imperator is revealed, it will no longer be just a matter of Daemons... But... is now the right time?
Yes... Let's get going then.
We'll go back to Margherita's. It's where our story began... and it's where it must end.
Such a beautiful voice, isn't it?
My name is Ciaccona, a—
Don't be scared. We're only here so that you can finish your game of hide and seek.
How-how about a little chat first? Something's troubling you, I can tell.
Ah, young ones today have so much talent... If I had the chance, I'd love to learn how to sing hymns.
You know, our Laureate here definitely knows how to sing hymns.
Well... what do you think a hymn should sound like?
No, not that...  I mean, how does it make you feel? Try to remember what you were feeling when you were singing alone in the corner just now.
Then hold onto that feeling. Let's rehearse again! You don't have anyone sitting in on your rehearsals, do you?
Well then, the Laureate and I will be the first to hear you perform!
As the forgotten songs rise once more, a tale of the past I shall tell...
This is the story of those who were accused of blasphemy.
They were once poets, singing songs for the Divinity and the Order. This is how their music became known to all.
Until one day, they stumbled upon a "truth" that could not be proven.
The Sentinel, whom we've always revered, is not what They seem.
The true Divinity granting us blessings is a majestic Celestial Steed that gallops across the high seas and soars through the skies.
And the teachings in our scriptures? Lies. Lies told to us by the Order.
Ask yourselves this. Why would Imperator, who once sent Rinascita voices from the heavens, allow Their people to suffer aboard those Pilgrim's Sails?
How could poets who once sang the praises of the Sentinel come to curse and resent Rinascita, a land they loved so much?
People of Rinascita!
Have you ever wondered why the Order forbids us from seeing their earliest scriptures?
Why did none of the Fools that boarded the Pilgrim's Sail ever thank the Sentinel?
Imperator and Ragunna have always stood united, yet lately... they seem to have vanished from our dreams. Why is that?
Is that so? And what are the charges this time, if I may ask?
Oh? The same old tricks again. Is this how the Order intends to keep the truth hidden?
Farewell, Sir Acolyte. Oh, and just a little tip for you. Keep your eyes on the audience below.
Let's see how much of the Order's hypocrisy these "empty words" can unveil...
I have faith the music of the Golden Anthem will one day echo across Ragunna's skies again!
So... what did you see in Raisa's melody?
This incident may have been the turning point for the Poets Society. Did you find any other clues?
Next time we venture into the Realm of Melodies, perhaps we should tie our hands together to stop you from getting lost.
If the Order's choir is pointing us to Whisperwind Haven, then that's where we'll find more clues. Let's go.
{PlayerName}, {PlayerName}! Are you alright?
Finally, I got you out of there! You must've been drawn into a different melody on our way out.
I see...
I don't know who you met, but Toccata is my family name.
As I said before... for family matters, let the past stay where it belongs.
Just call me Ciaccona! We don't need to overcomplicate things.
Hm. Well, we have made some progress. The melody we were in gave me a hint on what to do next.
Yes. Seeing as they banished the Golden Anthem, we might find more clues in the Cathedral of Mercury.
Music and poetry are born from kindness and hope... yet now they've been twisted to create these monstrosities...
Whoever is behind this won't just sit back and watch.
...The thought of confronting them is already inspiring me to write a ballad! What should I call it? Which instruments would fit...
If we find the <te href=800012>Melody</te> connected to the Poets Society, I can bring the past into clearer focus...
I've got it, {PlayerName}. I know a place where they've definitely left their mark.
Let's go! Onward, to new melodies!
Oh, what splendid singing. Even the Laureate was enraptured!
Yes, a hymn isn't just a few lines of history. It is shaped by your feelings and experiences.
A hymn may differ in each person's eyes, but its resonance and meaning are universal.
I hope you'll give the audience a spectacular performance on the day of the ceremony.
Exactly. Someone used the story of the Golden Anthem to stir the public's curiosity. This is what created the Daemons.
Are you from a noble family? The city's elite have a habit of siding with the Order.
...I see it differently.
Even if they met their end falling into the abyss, they still had a chance to glimpse the sky and spread their wings.
I really admire the wind. It's free to go anywhere without restraint. If only it could carry my poems into the hands of everyone across the world.
Congratulations, {PlayerName}. This victory is truly yours to claim.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: Haha, looks like the legend of the very first Apex Duelist won't end with me.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: Haha, but no. That path isn't meant for me. I'm a wandering bard, remember?
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: I don't become the legend. I walk beside it, witness it, weave it into song, and most of all, tell its story.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: And the hero of this story is you, {PlayerName}, the Apex Duelist.
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige

lines.text: Look forward to the song I'll write, a song born from this duel, {PlayerName}!
lines.quest_name: All Out! Towards the Peaks of Prestige
